assertions.details: Observed status=ok, provider=codex, listen=127.0.0.1:18318, upstream=http://127.0.0.1:18319 on the reachable fixture and the same local health response with upstream=http://127.0.0.1:18339.
assertions.summary: Health checks returned 200 with local proxy identity fields both with the mock upstream and with an unreachable local upstream port.

assertions.details: The stats call returned HTTP 200 with a JSON payload and the mock log remained empty (request_count=0), confirming the operator surface is read-only and local.
assertions.summary: The local /v0/runtime/stats surface returned 200 without auth and did not create any upstream mock traffic.

assertions.details: Before and after mutating the state file on disk, /v0/runtime/stats continued to report active_account_name=runtime-valid and auth_health.state=valid for the running process while exposing bind_address, upstream_base_url, account_count, auth_health, request_counters, and last_refresh.
assertions.summary: The runtime stats payload exposed the expected stable schema and stayed pinned to runtime-memory state after the disk state file was mutated externally.

assertions.details: Observed auth_health.state values: fallback-key -> static with source=config_fallback_key and no active_account_name; static saved account -> static with source=active_account; valid fixture -> valid; expiring fixture -> expiring_soon; expired fixture -> expired; malformed expiry fixture -> unknown with metadata_state=malformed.
assertions.summary: Representative fixtures produced explicit auth-health states for fallback static, saved static, valid, expiring_soon, expired, and malformed unknown cases.

assertions.details: Initial stats showed all request counters at 0 and last_refresh.kind=unknown with null timestamps; after two successful requests the counters rose; after restarting the proxy with the same fixture they reset to zero again.
assertions.summary: Fresh runtime counters started at zero, last-refresh metadata was null-like before any attempt, and a restart cleared in-memory stats after traffic.

assertions.details: Starting from zero, one GET /v1/models and one POST /v1/chat/completions produced total_proxied_requests=2 and successful_upstream_responses=2, with two corresponding mock upstream calls.
assertions.summary: Successful supported proxy requests incremented both total_proxied_requests and successful_upstream_responses.

assertions.details: With an expired refreshable account, the next proxied request returned 502, auth_refresh_attempts/auth_refresh_failures incremented, last_refresh.kind became failure, and the local upstream mock saw zero calls. This confirms the pre-forward refresh attempt, but successful local stubbed refresh remains blocked because the available config surface has no OAuth token endpoint override.
assertions.summary: A proactive refresh attempt was observable before forwarding, but the success path could not be validated because the runtime cannot be redirected from the user surface to a local OAuth token stub.

assertions.details: The local mock forced one upstream 401 and the proxy recorded upstream_401_count=1 with a single proxied request, but the refresh attempt failed with a 502 response and the mock saw no /oauth/token call. A successful refresh-and-retry path therefore remains blocked under the current user-surface configuration.
assertions.summary: The 401-triggered refresh path is visible, but the success retry case cannot be validated locally because the runtime cannot point refresh traffic at a local OAuth token stub from the user surface.

assertions.details: The client received HTTP 502 with a local JSON error after the mock upstream returned 401, while /v0/runtime/stats showed total_proxied_requests=1, upstream_401_count=1, auth_refresh_attempts=1, auth_refresh_failures=1, and last_refresh.kind=failure with a failure timestamp/message.
assertions.summary: A 401-triggered refresh failure remained visible to the operator surface and did not masquerade as success.

assertions.details: POST /v1/files returned HTTP 404 with an error naming /v1/files and the supported routes list; successful_upstream_responses and total_proxied_requests stayed unchanged across the before/after stats captures.
assertions.summary: Unsupported /v1/* routes returned a clear 404 JSON error and did not change success counters.

blockers: VAL-HTTP-007 and VAL-HTTP-008 remain blocked for success-path validation because proxypilot-rs exposes no user-surface config knob to redirect refresh token exchange from the hardwired OAuth endpoint to a local stub.
overallSummary: Validated 10 HTTP runtime assertions: 8 passed and 2 were blocked (refresh-success paths VAL-HTTP-007 and VAL-HTTP-008).
